section: Chapter 1: The Story of Village Palampur

questions.question: What is the primary production activity in Palampur?
questions.explanation: Farming is the main activity, with 75 per cent of the working population dependent on it for their livelihood[cite: 205, 255].

questions.question: What are the four requirements for the production of goods and services?
questions.correct_answer: Land, labour, physical capital, and human capital[cite: 231, 232, 233, 236, 244].
questions.explanation: These four elements are collectively known as the factors of production[cite: 250].

questions.question: Define 'Fixed Capital' with examples.
questions.correct_answer: Tools, machines, and buildings that can be used in production over many years[cite: 238].
questions.explanation: Examples range from simple tools like a farmer's plough to sophisticated machines like generators and computers[cite: 238].

questions.question: What constitutes 'Working Capital'?
questions.correct_answer: Raw materials and money in hand[cite: 241].
questions.explanation: Unlike fixed capital, these items are used up during the production process[cite: 242].

questions.question: What is 'Multiple Cropping'?
questions.correct_answer: Growing more than one crop on a piece of land during the year[cite: 284].
questions.explanation: It is the most common way to increase production on a given piece of land[cite: 285].

questions.question: How did the Green Revolution impact wheat yields in Palampur?
questions.correct_answer: Yields increased from 1300 kg to 3200 kg per hectare[cite: 343, 344].
questions.explanation: The use of High Yielding Varieties (HYV) seeds led to a large increase in wheat production[cite: 345].

questions.question: What are the environmental drawbacks of modern farming methods?
questions.correct_answer: Loss of soil fertility and depletion of the water-table[cite: 369, 370].
questions.explanation: Chemical fertilizers kill soil bacteria and pollute groundwater, while tubewell irrigation lowers the water table[cite: 370, 397, 398].

questions.question: How is land distributed among the families of Palampur?
questions.correct_answer: Unequally: 150 families are landless, 240 families have small plots, and 60 families have large/medium plots[cite: 407, 410, 421].
questions.explanation: Large farmers may have land extending over 10 hectares or more, while small plots are often less than 2 hectares[cite: 410, 422].
questions.source_section: How is land distributed between the farmers of Palampur?

questions.question: Why do small farmers face 'great distress' to arrange capital?
questions.correct_answer: They must borrow at high interest rates from moneylenders or large farmers[cite: 485, 487].
questions.explanation: Unlike large farmers who have their own savings, small farmers often lack surplus and face tough loan conditions[cite: 492, 496, 497].

questions.question: Give examples of non-farm activities in Palampur.
questions.correct_answer: Dairy, small-scale manufacturing, shopkeeping, and transport[cite: 547, 567, 585, 601].
questions.explanation: Only 25 per cent of the people working in Palampur are engaged in these non-agricultural activities[cite: 546].

notes.title: Factors of Production
notes.content: Production requires land, labour, physical capital (fixed and working), and human capital[cite: 231, 232, 233, 236, 244]. These are organized together to produce goods and services[cite: 250].

notes.title: Modern Farming vs. Traditional
notes.content: Modern methods use HYV seeds, chemical fertilizers, and pesticides, which require more water and capital than traditional methods[cite: 304, 307, 310, 311, 312].

notes.title: Labour Dynamics
notes.content: Small farmers provide their own labour, while medium and large farmers hire farm labourers who are often paid less than the government-set minimum wage[cite: 444, 445, 472, 474].

facts.fact: Raiganj is a big village located 3 kms from Palampur[cite: 208].
The standard unit of measuring land is the hectare[cite: 262].
The minimum wage for a farm labourer set by the government was Rs 300 per day in March 2019[cite: 472].
Only 24 out of every 100 workers in rural India are engaged in non-farm activities[cite: 636].
